string 2: So, you really don't know anything about the Digital World?
Nope. The first thing I remember...
...is when Inori saved me. Everything before that, well...

Talk about unusual! In fact, this is the first I've ever 
heard of such a thing.

Digimon are all born and raised in the Digital World.
At least, as far as I know.
...Oh, good morning, {player}.
Minervamon's been telling me about all kinds of neat stuff.
Like the Digital World... And that it's where Digimon usually live.

But I wonder, though... How come I didn't know about any of
this stuff?
Aegiomon...

If you really want to know, why not join me when I go back
to my world?
Join you?
No need to be so on guard. Consider it a little vacation.

But, of course... I can't go back there until I find what
I'm looking for here...
...Got it. I'm in, then! I'll do whatever I can to help!
Hey, {player}, about that thing Minervamon is looking for...
Why don't we ask that lady about it?

You mean that Dr. Simmons character? Don't you think she was a little...
y'know... nutty?

Maybe, but she's super curious about Digimon. That's why I
figure she'd be willing to help us out.

Think we can find her if we go look for that car in the
park again? C'mon. Let's head over there!
I'm down for checking out Shinjuku Park.{next}
Hopefully, she has information about the Shinjuku Inferno.
Sure. Gotta keep an eye on the class-A anomalies.
Yeah, Dr. Simmons might help us out!
{player}, Minervamon, let's go!
Um, what do you mean by that?
"Ano...maly"? What the heck is that?
Hmm. Sounds like we have to head to Akihabara, then...
Where in Akihabara?{next}
But the information is uncertain...
To go check out some action figures?
That guy said something about hunting for parts, right?

Then I bet we should start with the shops under the overpass.
Anyway, let's head over and see what we can find.

But staying here won't help anything, either. We ought 
to go check out some other spots, if you ask me.

What, no! I mean, yeah... there are a ton of them
around there... but no.

It's just that staying here won't help anything. We ought to
go check out some other spots, if you ask me.

We need to take the train if we're going to Akihabara.
Let's head to Shinjuku Station.
This one sparks joy... This one does not spark joy...
...Hmm?

Oh, it's you folks. Thinking of cooperating with my
research again?
We need your help.{next}
What are you doing?

Well that's a coincidence. We, too, need someone to help.
Still, let's start with your problem first.

Oh, I'm working on a project right now, but I'm having a
little trouble.
I was just trying to figure out how to deal with it.
Yes, yes, I see... Quite intriguing.

So, you're looking for something Digimon-related, yes? 
Our research might be of some use to you.

Actually, our team has been making strides on a certain
project lately: radar that detects phase electrons.
But, currently, all it can do is detect them.

Eventually, however, we're also hoping to use the electromagnetic
waves it emits to "interact" with phase electrons.
But here's the problem.

We've suddenly lost contact with the squad in charge of
installing one of the five small base stations.

If you help me look into it, I'll give you a prototype detector. 
It ought to help you find what you're looking for.
All right. We'll take the job.{next}
Tell me more about this prototype detector.
Public safety is short staffed too, huh?

It's settled! Head to the Higashi-Shinjuku subway. That's where
the squad should be.

Given how we lost contact, I'm guessing the squad isn't 
exactly having a spa day over there. Tread carefully.

Think of it as radar that detects the presence of phase electrons.
It might help you find what you're looking for.

It's still in the prototype phase, though, so it's pretty 
bulky, to be sure.

But if I use the parts I found here, I'm certain I can make it
smaller than it has any business being.

Be it manpower or funds, we're always short on something. 
Not many folks rushing to join the "anomaly squad," you see.

Which is why I'm willing to take any help I can get, provided it's a
fair exchange, of course.
Not a bad deal, right? What do you say?

Dr. Monica Simmons... A cursory look into her background 
shows just how famous she is in her field.

That being said, she apparently suddenly vanished from the
realm of academia after a certain point.

Once she was in public safety, personal information and
records became hard to find. Everything was confidential.

In exchange for giving up her social status, she probably
chose to have access to state-level classified information.

A genius who left the public stage... Somehow, that brings
Dr. Yuki to mind.
Damn it all!
We're here on Dr. Simmons' orders.{next}
Are you all right?
Her orders?
Anyway, our squad can't do anything against a monster like that.

Wait... I remember you guys! What are you doing here?! This
area is off-limits!

The monster stole the device's activation switch... and ran off in 
there.
We'll go take a look.{next}
What kind of "monster," exactly?
I hate to have to ask civilians for help, but please!

It's that same strange creature as before. Looks like we're
just gonna have to count on you for this...
Where are we?
This charge in the air... I'd say we're in the Outer Dungeon.

It's an unstable space that's in between the Digital World
and your world.

And I'm guessing the "monster" up ahead is a Digimon.
Let's stay sharp.
You must be nervous...{next}
Don't be scared. I'm right beside you.{next}
Keeping citizens safe is an agent's duty.{next}

I'm okay. Really, I am. The jitters I felt before are all pretty
much gone.

After all, I've got a new, dependable friend in a funny
outfit by my side.

It's like I've got an anime character watching out for me. 
"Agent Alpha" was my hero, you know.

Anyway, I just wanted to say that I'm relying on you. 
No need to worry about me, let's hurry ahead.
Nyahaha! I got my hands on a real prize this time!

More! I have to go swipe more stuff off of them! You should've
seen the looks on their faces! Nyahaha!
Give back what you stole.{next}
What're you doing here?{next}
Sounds like you're having fun. Mind if we join in?{next}

Nuh-uh, no way! If you want it, you're going to have 
to take it! Nyahaha!

None of your business! Maybe I'm just enjoying the look on
weaklings' faces when I snatch away their precious things!

You humans are no match for us! If you don't like it, why
don't you try to do something about it?!

The heck are you saying?! You guys are just stupid humans!
No way I'm going to let the likes of you in on my fun!

It's because you humans are so weak that we play around
with you like this. Toys should know when to keep quiet!

We should go give it back to the public safety guy right
away.

We'll handle the cleanup from here. Dr. Simmons is already
back at the public safety vehicle in Shinjuku Park.

Thanks again for all your help. We'll close this area off to
the public again.

The name's Shota Kuroi. I'll be sure to return the favor
sometime.
I'll escort you back to Dr. Simmons' location.
I've heard the report. Fantastic work out there.
I've made massive leaps in my research, thanks to you.

Time to hold up my end of the bargain. That communicator of 
yours... May I see it for a moment?

There! I've installed that miniaturized detector I proposed 
earlier into your communicator. Works just like I explained.
But I have to ask...

Where in the world are you from, anyway? That device isn't
tech you'd find in any retail store, that's for sure.

I couldn't wrap my head around most of the functionality. 
What would you even use something like that for?
I'm afraid I can't answer that.{next}
Its functionality is essential for preventing a global collapse.
..."Can't," you say? All right. I won't push it.
Clamming up, huh?
Like a secret agent who's been through interrogation training...
If you can't tell me, then just say so. I don't mind leaving it at that.

If that were true, it would be one heck of a feature, to be sure...
Sounds like something out of an anime.

And don't get me wrong. I'm into all that stuff... but, still, reckless
lies never help.

Phase electron lifeforms are being pursued by the government
agencies of all the developed nations, after all...

One or two spies in the mix would hardly be a surprise. The 
only thing that astonishes me... is the beauty of your device.

Whoever built that marvel... They're a genius, maybe even
more gifted than yours truly.

I'm fascinated by you...! Where you come from, and who's 
backing you.

I imagine you're not one to easily divulge your secrets...
Nevertheless, you'll find I don't give up easily.

I think this might be the start of a long and fruitful
relationship. *giggle*
